title: Protocol: antimicrobial resistance trial deficit in Africa
primary_estimand: Clinical-Crisis Index comparing Africa's AMR death share to its AMR trial share
body: This cross-sectional registry study will quantify the gap between antimicrobial resistance clinical trial activity and AMR mortality burden across Africa using ClinicalTrials.gov data. We will query ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 for interventional trials matching antimicrobial resistance, drug resistant, MRSA, ESBL, carbapenem resistant, or MDR across ten regions including four African countries, the United States, India, China, Brazil, and the United Kingdom. The primary outcome is the Clinical-Crisis Index, defined as Africa's share of global AMR deaths divided by its share of global AMR trials, using IHME 2019 mortality estimates as the numerator benchmark. Secondary analyses will classify Africa's AMR trials by target pathogen, intervention type distinguishing novel antibiotics from stewardship and diagnostics, sponsor category separating industry from academic and public funders, and phase distribution. Supplementary queries will separately enumerate antibiotic stewardship and diagnostic-infection trials in Africa versus the United States. No patient-level data will be used. Limitations include registry dependence, inability to capture trials registered only on WHO ICTRP or national registries, and reliance on published mortality estimates rather than contemporaneous surveillance.
ai_transparency: LLM assistance was used for drafting and language editing. The author reviewed and edited the manuscript and takes responsibility for the final content.

checks.name: methods described
checks.detail: Protocol describes API query strategy, CCI definition, pathogen and intervention classification, sponsor categorisation, and supplementary stewardship/diagnostic queries.

checks.name: outcomes specified
checks.detail: Primary (CCI) and secondary (pathogen, intervention type, sponsor, phase, stewardship gap, diagnostic gap) outcomes specified.
